name: Potato Leek Spinach Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 large leeks, white and tender green parts only
3/4 pound mushrooms (I used plain white button mushrooms)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and coarsely chop the potatoes.
2. Trim the leeks, slice them in half lengthwise, and separate under running water to remove all sand and dirt. Coarsely chop the white and tender green parts.
3. Clean and quarter the mushrooms.
4. Place all ingredients in a large pot and just cover with water. Add salt, about 2 teaspoons, and bring to boil.
5. Simmer until the potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
6. Ladle half of the cooked potato mixture into a food processor and add half of the spinach. Process until smooth and thick.
7. Repeat with the remaining potato mixture and spinach.
8. Add about 1 tablespoon butter buds for flavor, if desired, and thin with skim milk until the soup reaches the desired consistency.
Serve hot. Makes about 6-8 servings (although 2 people ate at least half of it at one sitting, along with fresh crusty French bread).
